seoTitle: How to Change the Logo on a QuickBooks Invoice
metaDescription: Update the logo on your QuickBooks invoices to keep branding consistent across customer communications, with tips for sizing it correctly.
intro: A consistent logo on your invoices reinforces your brand and builds trust with clients, especially during a rebrand or when refreshing your marketing materials. This guide covers uploading a new logo in QuickBooks and getting it to display properly on your invoices.

steps.heading: Open your QuickBooks invoice settings
steps.body: Navigate to the settings area where invoice customization options, including your logo, are managed.

steps.heading: Upload your new logo file
steps.body: Select and upload the updated logo image you want to appear on customer-facing invoices.

steps.heading: Adjust logo sizing and placement
steps.body: Resize or reposition the logo as needed so it fits cleanly within the invoice template.

steps.heading: Preview the invoice before sending
steps.body: Check a preview of the invoice to confirm the new logo looks correct before it goes out to clients.

steps.heading: Save changes for future invoices
steps.body: Once confirmed, save the update so every new invoice automatically uses the new logo going forward.

faq.question: Will changing my logo affect past invoices already sent?
faq.answer: No, updating your logo only affects new invoices going forward — previously sent invoices keep the logo that was in place at the time.

faq.question: What image format works best for a QuickBooks invoice logo?
faq.answer: A clear, properly sized image file works best; QuickBooks will typically guide you on supported formats during upload.

faq.question: Can I remove the logo entirely from my invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, the same settings that let you add a logo also let you remove it if you prefer a text-only invoice.

faq.question: Does this feature work the same for automated recurring invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, once your logo is updated in settings, it applies to automated and recurring invoices as well.
